warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ee7g","text":"Make sure to be aware of the safety regulations associated with handling radioactive material!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":94}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mix the purified recombinant KaiC protein in reaction buffer with [y-32P]ATP as described in the table below. If you want to determine the influence of KaiA, you will have to prepare samples with and without KaiA. You will need a master mix for all aliquots (timepoints) you want to analyze (e.g. 5) plus 1 additional aliquot (e.g. master mix for 6). Work on ice! %s %s

Briefly spin down the reaction mixture using a bench-top centrifuge, take a 10 µl aliquot and mix with 2.5 µl 5 X SDS-PAGE buffer to stop the reaction (timepoint zero). Store the aliquot at -20 °C and immediatly proceed with the next step.
Incubate the remaining reaction mixture at the appropriate temperature (e.g. 30 °C for KaiC from Synechococcus elongatus PCC 7942).
Take samples at different timepoints (e.g. 0.75, 1.5, 3, 22 hours) as described in step 2.
Separate proteins and stain them with Coomassie brilliant blue as described in Protocol LowC SDS-PAGE .
Dry gels for 2-3 hours under vacuum at 60°C.
Expose gels to an autoradiography screen and analyze signals using the Fujifilm FLA-3000.
Scan dried gels to document Coomassie staining of the proteins.
creator: Anika Wiegard
